calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Continued To Aug-19-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Physical Intelligence, Inc.'s unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Matthew D. Gorman Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Matthew D. Gorman has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) It is not sufficient for counsel to simply identify the number of cases in which PHV appearance has been authorized. (See Gorman Decl. para. 7.) The requirements are quite straightforward and set out clearly in the Rule of Court. Counsel requesting discretionary leave to appear in an action should take care to fulfill them.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Physical Intelligence, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

In Gamboa v. Northeast Community Clinic (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 158, the court describes the three-step process to establish the existence of an agreement to arbitrate. The first step requires the party seeking arbitration to carry the initial burden of presenting prima facie evidence of a written agreement to arbitrate the controversy. "A party seeking arbitration can carry its initial burden by attaching a copy of the arbitration agreement purportedly bearing the opposing party's signature. The party seeking arbitration need not follow the normal procedures of document authentication and need only allege the existence of an agreement and support the allegation as provided in rule [3.1330]." (Ramirez v. Charter Communications, Inc. (2024) 102 Cal.App.5th 821, 831.) "If that initial burden is met, the second step requires the party opposing arbitration to carry the burden of producing evidence to challenge the authenticity of the agreement. If the opposing party meets the burden of producing sufficient evidence, the third step requires the party seeking arbitration to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that the parties formed a valid contract to arbitrate their dispute." Ramirez v. Golden Queen Mining Co., LLC (2024) 102 Cal.App.5th 821, 830 (cleaned up).) "If the opposing party meets its burden of producing evidence, then in the third step, the moving party must establish with admissible evidence a valid arbitration agreement between the parties. The burden of proving the agreement by a preponderance of the evidence remains with the moving party." (Gamboa, 72 Cal.App.5th at 165-166.) Where Defendant does not present evidence that plaintiff had actual notice of the terms of service, defendant is required to establish contract formation on a constructive or inquiry notice theory. (Windsor Mills, Inc. v. Collins & Aikman Corp. (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 987.)

Defendant caried its initial burden presenting prima facie evidence of a written agreement to arbitrate the controversy. (See Walling Decl., para 4, Ex. A, section 11.A.) Plaintiff has failed to make any step two showing. Thus, the court finds the parties agreed to arbitrate. The agreement appears to cover the disputes at issue. (See FAC, passim.) Plaintiff has not established a defense to enforcement.

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's claims against Defendant Physical Intelligence, Inc. are ordered to arbitration and this action is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration proceedings.

In light of the order compelling arbitration and staying the action, as well as Defendant's failure to give proper notice of a motion to dismiss claims, this court declines to act on Defendant's request for an order dismissing certain claims.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn: Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
